title: LLM Jailbreaking: Attacks and Defenses

modules.title: What Jailbreaking Actually Is: Beyond the Pop-Culture Framing
modules.sub: Defining jailbreaking as a serious, measurable security threat
modules.description: Learners reframe jailbreaking from a hobbyist novelty into a professionally defined attack class with real organizational risk. They examine how jailbreaks differ from other AI vulnerabilities and map the threat to enterprise security frameworks. By the end, learners can articulate a precise, defensible definition of LLM jailbreaking to technical and non-technical stakeholders.

modules.title: Taxonomy of Jailbreak Techniques: Prompt Injection, Role-Play Exploits, and Multilingual Bypasses
modules.sub: Cataloguing and classifying the core jailbreak attack families
modules.description: Learners work through a structured taxonomy of jailbreak methods, analyzing how prompt injection, persona-based exploits, and language-switching attacks each manipulate model behavior differently. They classify real-world jailbreak samples into the taxonomy and assess the relative risk each technique poses in enterprise contexts. This builds the pattern-recognition vocabulary needed for effective red-teaming and defense design.

modules.title: How Jailbreaks Are Weaponized in Real Threat Campaigns
modules.sub: Tracing jailbreak techniques from proof-of-concept to active exploitation
modules.description: Learners analyze documented threat campaigns where jailbreaks were operationalized to extract sensitive data, generate harmful content, or subvert AI-powered business workflows. They map attacker objectives, entry points, and escalation paths to understand how theoretical vulnerabilities become operational risks. The module culminates in learners producing a threat narrative that connects a jailbreak technique to a plausible attack scenario in their own environment.

modules.title: Red-Teaming LLMs: Hands-On Attack Simulation Exercises
modules.sub: Executing structured adversarial probing against live LLM deployments
modules.description: Learners conduct guided red-team exercises against sandboxed LLM environments, applying jailbreak techniques from the taxonomy to identify exploitable weaknesses. They document successful bypass attempts, record model responses, and evaluate which system configurations are most vulnerable. The output is a structured red-team findings report that can serve as input to a real remediation workflow.

modules.title: Defense Architecture: Guardrails, Output Filtering, and System Prompt Hardening
modules.sub: Designing layered technical controls to resist jailbreak exploitation
modules.description: Learners design a multi-layer defense architecture by selecting and configuring guardrails, input validation rules, output filters, and hardened system prompts appropriate to their deployment context. They evaluate trade-offs between security strictness and model usability, making deliberate design decisions rather than applying defaults. The module produces a documented defense configuration that addresses the vulnerabilities identified during red-teaming.

modules.title: Evaluating LLM Security Before and After Deployment
modules.sub: Building repeatable assessment processes for LLM security posture
modules.description: Learners design a security evaluation framework that can be applied both pre-deployment and as an ongoing post-deployment audit process. They select appropriate metrics, test case categories, and pass/fail criteria to produce assessments that are reproducible and stakeholder-readable. The result is a practical evaluation checklist and scoring rubric tailored to their organization's LLM use cases.

modules.title: Incident Response When an LLM Is Exploited
modules.sub: Responding decisively when a jailbreak attack succeeds in production
modules.description: Learners work through an LLM exploitation scenario and build an incident response playbook covering detection, containment, forensic logging review, and stakeholder communication. They make triage decisions under simulated time pressure, determining when to isolate the model, escalate internally, or notify affected parties. The module delivers a reusable IR playbook specific to LLM jailbreak incidents that integrates with existing security operations processes.

synopsis: Jailbreaking is no longer a hobbyist curiosity — it is an active attack vector used to extract harmful content, bypass enterprise policy controls, and manipulate AI-powered applications into unintended behavior. This course gives security professionals a structured understanding of the jailbreak threat landscape and the technical controls needed to harden LLM deployments against real-world exploitation.
rationale: Organizations are deploying LLMs into production systems without a coherent security model for adversarial prompt abuse, and existing curriculum stops at definitional awareness rather than actionable red-team and hardening techniques.
learning_outcome: Learners can execute a structured red-team assessment of an LLM deployment, identify exploitable jailbreak pathways, and implement layered mitigations including system prompt hardening, input validation, and output filtering.
source_signals: Adversarial poetry as a universal single-turn jailbreak mechanism in LLMs
Defending LLMs against Jailbreaking Attacks via Backtranslation
Hacking AI is TOO EASY (this should be illegal)
